rag_rules: Fix type mismatches between assumed and actual port types by aligning helper declarations with expression types.
Ensure AGREE contracts are placed in valid component type or implementation scopes based on visibility and binding requirements.
generic_rules: Use only visible AADL features or helper declarations within the same AGREE annex to avoid unresolved references.
Validate that AGREE scalar type names (int, real, bool) match the expected types in declarations and expressions.
Place component type contracts in component type scopes and implementation bindings in implementation scopes.
Avoid referencing implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Component type annexes are for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations; implementation annexes for bindings.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or types not present in the analyzed model.
Do not reference ports or features that are unreachable or of an inadequate kind in AGREE contracts.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about unreachable or inadequate port references
Component type and implementation structure extraction
